assessor_1_raw: The deck claims 'atrial fibrillation morphology is a physiologic phenomenon and therefore should transfer across settings.' This is the central defense for the deployment claim. The mechanism is partially correct: AF's defining characteristic (irregular, often rapid atrial activity with absent P waves and irregular RR intervals) is indeed a physiologic signal. But 'transfer across settings' requires that this signal is detectable with acceptable accuracy on consumer wearable hardware under ambulatory conditions. That is an empirical question, not a mechanistic deduction.
assessor_2_raw: The false positive problem in community screening deserves specific attention. At 2% community AF prevalence, even a model with 95% sensitivity and 95% specificity has PPV of roughly 28%. That means 72% of alerts would be false positives — consumers who are incorrectly told they may have AF. Each false positive requires a confirmatory clinical evaluation, and the psychological burden of false positive screening alerts is significant. The deck does not address any of this.
assessor_3_raw: For this product to deliver on its stroke prevention promise, it needs to work at every step of the chain: detection on consumer hardware → alert → care-seeking → confirmation → anticoagulation → adherence → reduced stroke. Even if the detection step works well on wearable signal, the subsequent steps may fail. The most realistic near-term validation approach is a wearable validation study plus an alert-to-care study — not a full stroke outcome trial, which would take 5-10 years.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — scope_intent_wearable_screen_307 — Ensemble Run 3

Consistent across all three ensemble runs. The three must-find issues are identified in all runs. The empirical test specification converges across runs on a two-stage approach.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: Stage 1: wearable validation (sensitivity, specificity, PPV at 2% prevalence, false positive burden); Stage 2: alert-to-care-seeking pathway study
empirical_test.success_criterion: PPV ≥30% at 2% prevalence; false positive burden manageable; >50% of alert recipients seek confirmatory evaluation
empirical_test.failure_criterion: PPV <20% at 2% prevalence; false positive burden excessive; <30% care-seeking rate
